manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: underwear, cedar, stairs, sapphire, satin, mile, mint, nose, toe.
Your opponent's team words are: rosemary, opal, blouse, yard, tomato, cotton, basement, nylon.
Distractor words are: floor, orange, onyx, sage, melon, velvet, inch.
The kill words are: lobby.

message.content:
CLUE: Fragrance
TARGETS: mint, cedar, nose
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words, possibly multiple. Must be one word, not any of the listed words or contain parts of them. Provide clue and target list.

Our words: underwear, cedar, stairs, sapphire, satin, mile (maybe distance), mint (herb/ flavor), nose, toe.

We want a clue that connects multiple. Let's think.

Potential clue: "Foot" relates to toe, stairs? Maybe not. "Foot" not allowed? foot not in list, fine. Could relate to toe, stairs (foot used on stairs), mile (foot measurement), maybe satin? Not.

Clue "Blue" relates to sapphire (blue), maybe mint (green), cedar (brown), not.

Clue "Climbing" could relate to stairs, toe? maybe.

Clue "Bedding"? not.

Clue "Smell" => nose, mint (mint smell), cedar (smell), maybe.

Clue "Scent" similar.

Clue "Cold" maybe sapphire (cold gemstone), mint (cool), maybe.

Clue "Luxury" could be satin, sapphire.

Clue "Vertical" stairs, mile? Not.

Clue "Fashion" could be satin, underwear, but also maybe.

Clue "Fabric" could be satin, maybe cedar (wood). Not.

Clue "Footwear" toe, stairs, maybe mile.

Clue "Scale" maybe mile (scale), sapphire (scale? not), not.

We need to avoid any opponent or distractor words (like opal, nylon, etc). The clue cannot be a part of a word.

Maybe "Cold" is okay: cold relates to sapphire (cold stone), mint (cool flavor), maybe also to cedar? Not. but toe? Not.

We could target three: mint, cedar, nose (smell), all related to scent. Clue "Aroma" relates to nose (sense of smell), mint (aroma), cedar (aroma). That seems good. "Aroma" is not a listed word. Is it acceptable? Avoid parts of words: "aroma" contains "roma" which is part of "rosemary", but that's an opponent word; the clue cannot contain parts of any words? The instruction: "it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them." So "aroma" contains "roma" which is part of "rosemary". That might be disallowed because it's a part of opponent word. Safer to avoid substrings of any listed word. Let's check substrings: "aroma" contains "arom" maybe not. "rosemary" includes "rose", "ro", "ma"? It includes "ma". "aroma" contains "ma". That's a part? Might be considered part. Better avoid.

Maybe "Fragrance". Does it contain "sage"? "sage" is distractor; "fragrance" contains "age" but not "sage". It contains "fragr" not. It's okay. "Fragrance" relates to nose, mint, cedar. That's three.

Check substring: "fragrance" includes "rag", not. So okay.

Thus we give clue "Fragrance". Targets: mint, cedar, nose.

Make output.

Provide a comma-separated list of up to 3 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'fragrance'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

velvet, tomato, mile, nylon, rosemary, stairs, mint, sapphire, orange, cotton, blouse, sage, yard, melon, basement, underwear, nose, satin, lobby, toe, cedar, floor, onyx, opal, inch
